results.title: Complaints | Canadian Association of Movers
results.content:
English
 Français

## Language switcher

 English
 Français

Welcome to The Canadian Association of Movers serving the Canadian moving industry since 1969.  |  CAM is Canada's only trade association representing moving and storage companies.

## Complaints

Complaints

Complaints

### Unhappy about your move?

Maybe your move didn't go as well as expected, with lost or damaged goods, late delivery, or a higher cost than quoted. Now you want to file a complaint.

The Canadian Association of Movers (CAM) strongly urges all parties involved in a moving dispute to keep the lines of communication open toward resolving the issue.

### Step 1 [...] ### Step 1

Before you consider complaining to any authority, the most important step to take is to contact the company that you booked your move with to allow the moving company to address the situation.

You can contact them by phone, email, or letter to outline what your issues are and your desired resolution for the dispute.  Your booking agent (the moving company you booked your move with) is your primary contact for questions about your move and any issues you have, including complaints and claims.

For example, if you booked with ABC Movers and XYZ Movers showed up for the move, you must address your issues through ABC.  Similarly, if you moved from Ireland to Canada and you booked your move through an Irish company, you must address your issues through the Irish company. [...] If the mover is a CAM member, CAM will:

 share the form (and any additional information you provide) with the CAM member for response and resolution.
 record the complaint.
 as necessary, act as facilitator between you and the CAM member..
 if appropriate, refer the matter to the affiliated van line for response and resolution.
 advise the consumer of action taken and responses received.

If the mover is NOT a CAM member, CAM:

 will NOT contact the mover.
 will record the complaint.
 may recommend a course of action.
 may provide contact information for governmental consumer agencies, the Better Business Bureau or other enforcement agencies.

\you can check to see if the mover is a CAM member by searching CAM's Member Directory.

results.title: How to Identify Red Flags When Researching Moving Company Reviews - VTRN Homes
results.content:
Balance is another important clue. Even the best movers will have a few unhappy customers. What matters is how the company responds. A respectful reply, an apology, or an effort to make things right says a lot about their professionalism. On the other hand, ignoring complaints or answering defensively should give you pause. 

Here are a few markers that usually point to a moving company you can trust:

 Reviews that describe honest pricing with no hidden fees.
 Customers noted that movers handled valuable or fragile items with care.
 A consistent pattern of positive experiences, not just a streak of perfect scores.
 Professional, constructive responses from the company when problems come up. [...] Consistent praise for fair pricing, careful handling, and respectful crews is a strong sign of reliability. On the other hand, repeated complaints about surprise fees, broken items, or poor communication are warnings you can’t afford to ignore.

Bottom line? Read carefully, cross-check your sources, and trust your instincts. A company that earns your confidence on paper is far more likely to deliver the kind of moving day you actually deserve.

About the Author: Preston Guyton is the founder of ez Home Search. He has been a real estate leader for over 20 years. Starting with a focus on South Carolina, he has helped coach and empower real estate professionals to achieve their full potential by meeting the needs of their local community.

# 0 Comments

### Submit a Comment Cancel reply [...] ### Unprofessional or Incomplete Documentation

Reviews that mention blank contracts, vague agreements, or missing paperwork (like the federally required “Your Rights and Responsibilities When You Move” booklet) should raise concern. Trustworthy movers provide clear, detailed documents.

### Unmarked Vehicles or Movers Without Identification

When past customers describe crews arriving in rental trucks with no logos, or movers in street clothes with no ID, it signals a lack of professionalism and accountability.

### Repeated Complaints About Hidden Fees or Poor Handling

One bad experience can happen anywhere, but when you see the same issues come up repeatedly, including: 

 Damaged items
 Surprise charges
 Belongings held hostage for more money

This is a pattern worth avoiding.

results.title: Fake reviews - Google Business Profile Community
results.content:
I would recommend suspending their Google My Business account before others are taken advantage of by this company. Extremely frustrating to trust a business based on reviews to later find out they were misrepresenting themselves via fake 5 star reviews. As you can see here, they're using a 3rd party review system and I can assure you, no moving company is getting that many 5 star reviews on a daily basis, and especially in this market.

Here's me finishing moving my stuff by myself, after having paid $1000+ to these movers. At the very least, I'd expect them to finish. As a result, I was charged extra by the apartment complex since my things were not out on the moving day. Horrible experience. Google has to figure out a solution to this problem.

results.title: Broken Arrow Movers | Broken Arrow Moving Company
results.content:
Zainab Bartley

### Third Move, Best Experience Yet

Honestly, this is the best company I’ve dealt with over the years for out of state moving (this is my third move in two years!). I would like to thank Frances who was really kind and personable when we first connected. Also, I wish I remembered the names of the movers who packed up my belongings because they were so kind and professional. There was a bit of a lapse in communication about delivering my belongings but I called Sandra who was on top of things right away. The movers that delivered my belongings were great as well! Above all, no items were broken and am very happy with their services. Highly recommend!

Dustin Marley

### Small Move from Main to New Mexico [...] My experience with Mini Moves exceeded expectations immensely. My movers were incredibly helpful and answered any questions I had along the way. I recently moved from an apartment to a house and had all my items stored in a storage unit. All I had to do was let them into the unit and they had done the rest in a little over an hour. They were super nice and respectful and moved my stuff with care. I also got a package delivered that was too heavy for me to carry and they moved it to the designated location which they did not have to do but were more than happy to help. The process of Mini Moves team was super easy and helpful. I had gotten text when they were able to deliver and the communication was incredible. My experience with Mini Moves was super stress free and I 10/10 recommend. I [...] First time customer. The upfront quoting process was very easy to do with Steve on the phone. It was very thorough and the charge was fair; I was ultimately charged a little bit less in the end which is always a welcome surprise. Insurance was offered but not aggressively pushed, up to each individual based on their belongings. Lue was in constant communication about paperwork, necessary logistics for the movers, and accurate timelines.

I moved from Dallas to Chicago. Dallas required a third party mover and they couldn’t have picked a better team. Their names escape me at this time unfortunately, but they were early, courteous, and efficient. They were done well in advance and took tremendous care of my belongings.
